citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Live leginfo confirms §39800(a) discretionary transport authority and §39800(b) definition of 'municipally owned transit system' cross-referencing PUC §24501. Matches claim. Added by Stats. 1999, Ch. 646, Sec. 14, eff. 1/1/2000 — matches ledger.

Live leginfo confirms the $10,000 threshold language, cross-reference to PCC §§20111 and 20112, and the 'may let the contract for the service to other than the lowest bidder' clause. Matches the Rebid-field claim verbatim. Added by Stats. 1999, Ch. 646.

Live leginfo confirms (a) 5-year cap on continuing services contract with joint-renewal carry-forward including rate-increase provisions; (b) 5-year lease/rental with 10-year option-to-purchase/cancel variant; (c) annual in-term renegotiation by mutual approval; (d) §17450 et seq. lease-compliance cross-reference. Matches Max-Term and Renewal field claims verbatim. Added by Stats. 1999, Ch. 646.

Live leginfo confirms 15-year zero-emission services cap and 15/20-year zero-emission lease cap with annual purchase/cancel option; LEA definition includes school district, county office of education, and charter school. Added by Stats. 2023, Ch. 445 (AB 579), eff. 1/1/2024 — matches ledger and claim verbatim.

Live leginfo: statutory base threshold is $50,000 with annual adjustment per subdivision (e) using Implicit Price Deflator for State and Local Government Purchases. Lowest-responsible-bidder rule confirmed. Last amended Stats. 2017 Ch. 395 (matches ledger). NOTE: The figure '$119,100 effective 1/1/2026' is the CDE-issued indexed adjustment per CDE Bid-Threshold Letter, NOT statutory text. Pack treats this nuance correctly (calls it 'cross-confirmed' externally), but ledger Currency cell could be read as conflating statutory amendment date with indexed-figure date.

Live leginfo confirms the cooperative/piggyback authority is limited to data-processing equipment, materials, supplies, equipment, automotive vehicles, tractors, and other personal property — not services. Matches the pack's adversarial finding that §20118 does not extend §39803's services cap.
Live leginfo confirms lease/lease-purchase authority for equipment/service systems (incl. schoolbuses); does not alter term caps in §39803. Matches Notes item (7).

Live leginfo confirms driver-safety requirements scope ('school-related pupil transportation for compensation') with parent/guardian and limited school-employee carve-outs. Added by Stats. 2023, Ch. 380 (SB 88); operative 7/1/2025 per §39882. Substantive content matches Notes item (11); ledger row does not state Ch. 380 specifically (minor hygiene).

URL attached in source-ledger is the Article 1 PORTAL link (codes_displayText), not a pinpoint to §39807.5 (codes_displaySection). The pinpoint URL https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=EDC&sectionNum=39807.5. resolves and confirms subdivision (e) prohibits transportation charges for 'individuals with exceptional needs as defined in Section 56026'. Pack paraphrase ('pupils with disabilities WHOSE IEP INCLUDES transportation as a related service') narrows the statutory scope: §56026 is the general 'individual with exceptional needs' definition tied to special-education eligibility, not specifically to IEPs that include transportation as a related service. Mild paraphrase drift on a non-load-bearing fee restriction.

URL 302-redirects to Radware/PerfDrive bot-protection wall under Claude Code's user agent; Firecrawl fallback unavailable (MCP token expired during audit). Pack's sourcesUnreachable list flags this page as 'bot-protection redirect; content cross-confirmed,' but source-ledger row (line 94) records it as accessed 2026-06-23 with no unreachable flag. Inconsistency between the JSON pack and the ledger.

citationVerifications.citation: California Legislative Information 2025-2026 bill search portal
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Portal resolves; Article 1 portal page (codes_displayText.xhtml?...article=1.) shows no post-2024 amendments to §§39800-39809.5. Negative-result current-law check is supported.

Page loaded; confirms $119,100 threshold effective 1/1/2026, derived from 3.772% Implicit Price Deflator adjustment per PCC §20111(a). Notably the PAGE LOADED for this audit despite pack's sourcesUnreachable list claiming it is bot-protection-redirected — bot detection appears intermittent / user-agent dependent.

mismatches: CDE Special Education Transportation Guidelines: scratch/california.json sourcesUnreachable lists it as bot-protection-redirected, but source-ledger.md row records it as accessed 2026-06-23 with no unreachable flag. Pick one.

§39807.5 URL in source-ledger row (line 95) is the Article 1 portal codes_displayText.xhtml link, not a §39807.5 pinpoint codes_displaySection.xhtml link. Other §-rows use pinpoint URLs — schema drift.

The pack's confidence is 'High' overall while one of the five fields (District Variance) is 'unresolved'. Per the spec, final 'High' is not assigned if the adversarial pass leaves an unresolved authoritative conflict; however, 'unresolved' here is a documented negative result rather than a sourced counterweight, so the spec's bar for blocking High is not strictly met. Still, presenting 'High' alongside 'Unknown' on one field is internally awkward and should be addressed explicitly (e.g., 'High on 4 fields; documented negative result on Field 5').

summary: California's load-bearing legal claims (Max Term §39803(a)/(b)/§39803.5; Rebid §39802 with $10,000 transportation-specific threshold; Renewal §39803(a) joint-mutual mechanic; Statutory Citation chain) re-verified verbatim on official leginfo on 2026-06-23. Special-education differentiation (none, with §39807.5 parent-fee carve-out) holds. District Variance properly preserved as 'Unknown' negative result. No defects rise to P1. Minor defects: (i) the §39807.5 source-ledger row attaches the Article 1 portal URL rather than a §39807.5 pinpoint URL (presentation/citation hygiene); (ii) the CDE Special Education Transportation Guidelines page is bot-protection-walled — flagged in scratch/california.json sourcesUnreachable but recorded as accessed in source-ledger.md (cross-file inconsistency); (iii) the ledger Currency cell on PCC §20111 conflates statutory amendment date (Stats. 2017 Ch. 395) with the 2026 indexed-figure date — substantively correct but typographically dense; (iv) §39807.5 prose paraphrase narrows 'individuals with exceptional needs (§56026)' to 'pupils with disabilities whose IEP includes transportation as a related service' — minor paraphrase drift on a non-load-bearing fee restriction. Overall verdict: Minor.

issues.evidence: Source-ledger row at source-ledger.md line 95 attaches the URL https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displayText.xhtml?lawCode=EDC&division=3.&title=2.&part=23.5.&chapter=1.&article=1. to '§39807.5'. That URL is the Article 1 PORTAL (codes_displayText.xhtml — lists all sections from 39800 to 39809.5), not a §39807.5 pinpoint (codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=EDC&sectionNum=39807.5.). Generic-portal-URL-not-pinpoint hygiene defect; the pinpoint URL does load and confirms the text.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Replace the URL in the §39807.5 source-ledger row with https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=EDC&sectionNum=39807.5. for consistency with the other §-rows.

issues.evidence: Notes prose states: '§39807.5 prohibits charging transportation fees to pupils with disabilities WHOSE IEP INCLUDES TRANSPORTATION AS A RELATED SERVICE.' Live §39807.5(e) reads: 'A charge under this section may not be made for the transportation of individuals with exceptional needs as defined in Section 56026.' §56026 defines 'individual with exceptional needs' as a special-education-eligible child generally — the §39807.5(e) carve-out is not conditioned on the IEP including transportation as a related service.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Adjust the paraphrase to track §39807.5(e)/§56026 wording: 'prohibits charging transportation fees to individuals with exceptional needs (as defined in §56026)' — preserves the non-load-bearing point without narrowing the statutory scope.

issues.field: Cross-file consistency on CDE Special Education Transportation Guidelines (cde.ca.gov/sp/se/lr/trnsprtgdlns.asp)
issues.evidence: scratch/california.json sourcesUnreachable includes 'CDE Bid Threshold 2025 page (bot-protection redirect; content cross-confirmed)' but DOES NOT list the SpEd Transportation Guidelines page; however, in this live re-audit the SpEd Guidelines page DOES 302-redirect to Radware bot-protection (validate.perfdrive.com) under Claude Code's user agent, while the Bid Threshold page DID load with full content. The source-ledger row for the SpEd Guidelines page records 'Accessed Date 2026-06-23' with no unreachable flag.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Reconcile: either confirm the SpEd Guidelines page is reachable via the authoring environment and add an access note; or mark it 'partially verified — bot-protection wall; substance cross-confirmed via federal IDEA / §56026 statutory text' in both the ledger row and the JSON pack. The Bid Threshold page should be moved off the JSON sourcesUnreachable list since it loads with full content.

issues.field: Confidence label vs. unresolved District Variance field
issues.evidence: scratch/california.json reports overall confidence 'High' while fieldVerdicts.districtVariance is 'unresolved'. The CLAUDE.md spec states 'final High confidence' should not be assigned where the adversarial pass leaves an unresolved authoritative conflict; the pack treats 'Unknown' as a documented negative result rather than an authoritative conflict, which is defensible — but the High/Unresolved juxtaposition is internally awkward.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Either qualify the overall confidence as 'High on four substantive fields; District Variance preserved as documented negative-result Unknown' or downgrade to 'Medium-High' to reflect the open Field 5.

issues.evidence: Source-ledger Currency cell reads 'Last amended Stats. 2017 Ch. 395; 2026 services threshold $119,100'. The $119,100 figure is NOT a statutory amendment — it is the CDE-issued annual index adjustment under PCC §20111(e). Mixing statutory amendment date and indexed-figure date in the same Currency cell can mislead a reader into thinking the statute itself was amended in 2026.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Split the Currency cell into 'Stats. amendment: 2017 Ch. 395 (SB 544)' and a separate Notes column item noting CDE's 2026 indexed services threshold of $119,100 per PCC §20111(e), to keep statutory currency cleanly separated from administrative indexing.

issues.evidence: scratch/california.json sourcesReVerified includes: '2026 PCC §20111 bid threshold ($119,100) cross-confirmed via SCLS, DWK, F3 Law, Lozano Smith, Liebert Cassidy Whitmore'. These are law-firm secondary summaries — proper pathfinders but not authority. The pack also cites the CDE Bid Threshold Letter (Tier 4) as the primary source, so the load-bearing authority IS official; the secondary-source list is acceptable as cross-confirmation only. Surface-level red flag worth noting.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Move the law-firm cross-confirmation list into a verification-notes 'pathfinders consulted' subsection rather than the sourcesReVerified roster, to keep authority-grade sources distinct from pathfinders.

issues.field: Negative search path scope for District Variance
issues.evidence: research-findings.md District Variance Exceptions entry (line 215) lists CDE bulletins, AG opinion DB, State Auditor, CSBA model policies BP 3540 / BP 3311, and large-district samples (Alameda USD, SFUSD). The negative search path does NOT mention searches of the California State Controller's Office K-12 Audit Guide or the Fiscal Crisis & Management Assistance Team (FCMAT) — both regularly publish on district procurement issues. Coverage is reasonable but not exhaustive on the public-finance-watchdog axis.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Optionally extend the negative search path note with FCMAT and State Controller's K-12 Audit Guide checks — not required to resolve the field, but it would tighten the 'Unknown' justification.
